prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿臀桥保持" (single-leg glute bridge hold) exercise, low-angle side view from ground level looking horizontally across the body. Person posture: Pilates-style demonstration: athletic person performing a single-leg glute bridge HOLD, photographed from a low ground-level camera looking horizontally across the body. The person is lying supine on the floor with shoulders, head, and arms flat on the ground. The right foot is on the ground with right knee bent 90 degrees, supporting the body. The hips are lifted into a bridge. The KEY visual: the LEFT LEG is FULLY STRAIGHTENED and held PARALLEL TO THE FLOOR like a horizontal pointer extending out from the body — left leg, hips, and right shoulder all align in one HORIZONTAL line that is parallel to the floor. The left toe points away from the body horizontally. Imagine an arrow lying flat on the floor, with the body forming the back of the arrow and the left leg pointing as the arrow's shaft. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: the left leg is NOT pointing toward the ceiling, NOT angled upward at all, NOT bent at the knee — it is COMPLETELY HORIZONTAL, parallel to the ground.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus maximus and hamstring group of the supporting (right) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: core muscles and erector spinae and gluteus medius for hip stability. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus of the supporting leg; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group of the supporting leg; "核心 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the core and erector spinae area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
